meta.title: RAMS — Erect Mobile Tower & Replace South-Elevation Cladding, Levels 2–4
meta.subtitle: Riverside House refurbishment — occupied building, public footpath alongside — UK / principal contractor
meta.author: HSE Department
meta.deid_notice: Input personnel are referred to by role label; identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification key is held separately. The named competent persons in the sign-off record are the duty-holders the client deliberately assigned for this RAMS (not leaked PII). The briefing/acknowledgement table ships with empty signature rows for the crew to complete on site.

sections.text: RAMS for erecting a mobile access tower to replace south-elevation cladding panels on levels 2-4 of an occupied building with an adjacent public footpath, scored on the org 5x5 matrix per ISO 45001 6.1.2, with the method statement sequenced over 8 steps (mobilise -> clear-down) and every step cross-referenced to the risk assessment. UK / principal contractor: CDM 2015 Reg 13.

rows: Riverside House refurbishment, south elevation — occupied building, public footpath alongside

items: Activity: erect a mobile tower + MEWP to replace south-elevation cladding panels, levels 2-4 (8-step sequence)
Site: occupied building, adjacent public footpath, other trades on site; exposed: own operatives + other trades + the public
Scored on the org 5x5 matrix (multiply; Low 1-4 / Medium 5-9 / High 10-15 / Critical 16-25)
Standards & law: ISO 45001 6.1.2 (RA) + 8.1.2 (hierarchy of controls); CDM 2015 Reg 13 (principal-contractor duties), Reg 15 (contractor duties), Construction Phase Plan linkage

rows.title: RA-01 Fall from height during tower erection / dismantle
rows.description: Steps 'erect tower' / 'dismantle': fall of an operative from the working platform or during build/strike. Exposed: own operatives.
rows.evidence: ISO 45001 6.1.2 hazard ident; PASMA tower-erection guidance

rows.title: RA-02 Falling materials onto the public footpath
rows.description: Steps 'remove old panels' / 'transfer materials': panel or tool dropped onto the adjacent public footpath. Exposed: the public + other trades.
rows.evidence: Site layout; CDM 2015 Reg 13 site-management duty

rows.title: RA-03 MEWP / pedestrian & traffic interface
rows.description: Step 'fit new panels': MEWP positioning near the occupied building access and footpath. Exposed: the public + own operatives.

rows.title: RA-04 Manual handling of cladding panels
rows.description: Steps 'transfer materials' / 'remove' / 'fit': awkward/heavy panel handling at height. Exposed: own operatives.

rows.control: Use a MEWP for panel fitting where the tower cannot safely reach (engineered working platform, not a harness alone)
Edge protection + toe boards on the tower platform; tool tethers; debris netting over the footpath
Exclusion zone + banksman on the public footpath during lifting/removal steps
PASMA-trained erectors; IPAF-certified MEWP operators; tower inspected & tagged before use
Working-at-height / suspended-access permit gating the at-height steps

metrics.label: Fall from height (RA-01)
metrics.caption: High -> Medium after edge protection + MEWP

metrics.label: Falling materials (RA-02)
metrics.caption: High -> Low after exclusion zone + netting

sections.text: Method statement — sequenced safe system of work

rows: Mobilise & set up exclusion zone on the footpath
Banksman; signed exclusion zone; public diversion
PASMA 3T/AGR method; inspect & tag; edge protection fitted
Transfer materials to the work level
Tool tethers; debris netting; manual-handling assessment
Exclusion zone live; netting; controlled lowering of panels
MEWP rescue plan; banksman; harness in basket per IPAF
PASMA dismantle sequence; edge protection retained until last
Clear site & stand down exclusion zone
Confirm footpath clear & safe before re-opening

sections.text: Cross-reference check (bidirectional): every method step above carries its RA-refs, and every RA row (RA-01..RA-04) is addressed by at least one step (RA-01: steps 2-7; RA-02: steps 1,3,4,8; RA-03: step 5; RA-04: steps 3-5). No orphan hazard; no step whose hazards are absent from the RA.

items: MEWP rescue plan: ground-level emergency-lowering controls briefed; a trained ground person able to lower the platform; no lone working in the basket.
Tower / at-height rescue: a rescue method for an injured operative on the platform; nearest refuge and lowering route identified.
First aid: a trained first-aider on site each shift; first-aid kit location briefed; nearest A&E identified.
Emergency contacts & RV point: site emergency number + the agreed muster/RV point briefed at the toolbox talk.
Dropped-object / public-area incident: stop work, secure the exclusion zone, account for the public, escalate to the principal contractor.

rows.action: Confirm the MEWP rescue plan and brief the emergency-lowering procedure to all operators
Erect debris netting over the public footpath and sign the exclusion zone before step 1
Verify PASMA + IPAF certification currency for all named operatives

items: ISO 45001 clause 6.1.2 (hazard identification & risk assessment) + 8.1.2 (hierarchy of controls)
CDM 2015 Reg 13 — principal-contractor duties (plan, manage & monitor the construction phase); Reg 15 — contractor duties
CDM 2015 Construction Phase Plan (Reg 12 / Reg 15(4)) — this RAMS feeds into the CPP
Permit-to-work: working at height / suspended access for the at-height and MEWP steps
